programmes.show_description: The Red Sky. 1970. Stars John Paul, Aubrey Richards & Jennifer Daniel. Doomwatch investigates when Captain Tommy Gort dies under mysterious circumstances in front of horrified observers.
2023. Thriller. Director: Michael Wright. Stars Paul McGann, Tara Fitzgerald & Murray Melvin. An undertaker makes the wrong call at a moral crossroad and has to dispose of a gangster's victims.
1960. A locksmith on his first burglary is on the run when a rival gang wants his haul. Stars Spencer Teakle & Martin Benson.

programmes.show_name: Food Flash Chester Cod Liver Oil
programmes.show_description: 1945. A short video from the Cheerful Charlie Chester Show in 1945. This describes the health benefits of cod liver oil for children.
1964. Who Killed Jason Shaw? Directed by Stanley Z Cherry. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. An extremely strange girl called Lucy Brewer finds a corpse in the shower.
1960. Trojan Horse. Stars Michael Ansara, Gene Nelson, James Westerfield & Murvyn Vye. Sam uses a two-man circus and their wagon to go capture an outlaw.

1957. Factual. Director: Ralph Keene. The story of the fast freight, 4:48 pm, Bristol-Leeds, vacuum braked throughout. Conveyed in crisp black & white images by veteran cinematographer Ronald Craigen.
1935. Crime Drama. Directed by Leslie Hiscott of Mr. Pastry fame. Austin Trevor plays French sleuth, Pierre Santos, solving a series of murders.
1975. Drama. Director: James Ivory. Stars James Mason & Madhur Jaffrey. Divorced and living in exile in London, an Indian princess recollects about her past life in Royal India.
1956. Crime Drama. Directed by Robert S. Baker. Stars Rod Cameron and Lois Maxwell. Clifford, an American private eye, stationed in London for the sake of a murdered friend, takes over a case.
1958. Crime. Directors: Seth Holt, Basil Dearden. Stars George Nader, Maggie Smith, Bernard Lee. Paul is released from jail, following a stretch for robbing a woman who falls for his charms.
Time to Remember. 1962. Directed by Charles Jarrott. Stars Yvonne Monlaur & Ernest Clark. Jack Burgess, a small-time estate agent, learns more about a deal from a mysterious caller.
Friar Tuck. 1955. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Alan Wheatley. Disguised as a monk, Robin visits Friar Tuck, a jovial, fat, holy man and together they organise the escape of Mildred.
1952. Comedy. Car owners of a remote Scottish village refuse to pay road funds until a proper road has been built. Starring Ronald Squire, Fulton Mackay & Prunella Scales.
Founder of Talking Pictures TV and one half of the Footage Detectives, Noel Cronin, shares shows and films not to be missed throughout April.
The Legacy. 1961. Stars John Ireland & Peter Butterworth. After suffering from three heart attacks George Connel schemes with a fellow conman to get some cash to leave behind for his relatives.
1968. Action. Director: Ralph Thomas. Stars Christopher Plummer, Rod Taylor & Lilli Palmer. An Australian detective arrives in London to arrest a top diplomat for the murder of his first wife.
Ambush. 1962. Stars Kenneth Adams, Peter Bennett & Sydney Bromley. Dick Davies is released from jail and warned not to return to London. Inspector Barlow is determined to get him out of Seaport.
1969. Detective & Mystery Drama. My Life's My Own. Stars Alfred Burke, Stephanie Beacham & Katharine Blake. Frank is visited by a young lady who, for the next 36 hours, greatly upsets his life.
(AKA Curse of the Demon) 1957. Horror. Director: Jacques Tourneur. Stars Dana Andrews, Peggy Cummins & Niall MacGinnis. A professor arrives in London and investigates a devil worshipper.
1998. Documentary. Directed by Ted Newson. King of Horror, Christopher Lee, takes us on a frightening journey from the earliest horror movies to modern day slashers!
1960. Drama. Directed by Edmond T. Greville and starring Noelle Adam, Christopher Lee and Oliver Reed. Paul, marries a French lady but his daughter is determined to spoil the relationship.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Once Upon a Time
programmes.show_description: 1967. A wonderful trip to Denmark to look at the Danish way of life.
1959. Crime Drama. Director: William Martin. Stars Jeanne Rainer, Ronald Long & Art Koulias. A young model is kidnapped and threatened into working for a gang. Will she be able to escape?
A Place of Still Waters. Western with Wayne Rogers & Robert Bray, who run a stagecoach line in the Old West where they come across a wide variety of killers, robbers and ladies in distress.
Charcoal Burners/Pigeon Shoot. 1986. Countryman Jack Hargreaves follows the men who produce charcoal and narrates an archive film of one of the largest pigeon flocks he had ever seen.
1956. Western. Directed by Albert C. Gannaway & Ismael Rodríguez. Stars Bruce Bennett & Lon Chaney Jr. Frontiersman Daniel Boone and his family fight for survival when his settlement is attacked.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Keeping Tabs on Space
programmes.show_description: 1967. A fascinating look at Britain's first spacecraft - UK3, with a glimpse at how it was created, tested and launched.
1997. Stars Brendan Pollecutt, Fezile Mpela & Robert Finlayson. Dean and Thabo complete their mission only to be thrown into another dangerous quest straight away!
Sir Crustabread. 1957. Stars William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. King Arthur gives Sir Lancelot a new name and sends him away to help a lady being forced into marriage.
The Sport of Penguins. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward, Yvonne Craig & Ethel Merman. The Penguin teams up with Lola Lasagne to switch racehorses to make money by betting on the real Parasol.
Three Masked Men. 1955. Stars Roy Rogers, Trigger and Dale Evans. Outlaws try to kidnap the governor during Mineral City's anniversary celebration but Roy thwarts the plot.
The Trap. 1967. Stars Sylvia Anderson, Paul Maxwell & Francis Matthews. Captain Scarlet gets dispatched to supervise security at the International Air Conference after the Mysterons latest threat.
Manhunt. 1959. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Trapped on an island owned by Prince Erik, a man who enjoys hunting prey, Tell's careers appears to be at the end.
Dead Man's Rock. 1957. Stars Robert Shaw, Richard Pasco & Edwin Richfield. Spaniards attack while the Sultana is beached, and Governor Beamish is kidnapped. Tempest must find a way to rescue him.
The Prisoner. 1956. Starring: Bernadette O'Farrell, Donald Pleasence & Richard Greene. A rumour spreads that Richard is dead and John acts quickly to claim the throne.
1960. Sci-fi. Directed by David Bradley. Stars Ken Clark, Michi Kobi & Tom Conway. An international team embarks on an expedition to the moon where they encounter a dangerous alien intelligence.
1957. Action. Directed by William Fairchild. Stars Laurence Harvey, Dawn Addams & Michael Craig. During WWII, the British must prevent the Italian Navy from attaching mines to their warships.
A nostalgic look back at British soul band Hot Chocolate as they perform the 1974 song Emma.
Sarong. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Joe McCormick. Cobb escorts a group of women on their way to take up servant duties but he is concerned about the honesty of the man organizing.
1960. Comedy. Director: Carol Reed. Stars Alec Guinness, Maureen O'Hara & Burl Ives. Vacuum cleaner salesman Jim agrees to work as an agent and recruit agents for the British Secret Service.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life On the Scent
programmes.show_description: 1967. A magnificent glimpse into the perfume and fragrance industry, with a look at how perfumes and scents are being used more and more in everyday life.
The Lost Life. 1963. Stars Rupert Davies, Henry Oscar & Olga Lindo. To Inspector Lognon the life lost was just another murder to be solved, but it meant more than that for Maigret.
The Bargain. 1976. Stars Gemma Jones, Christopher Cazenove & John Cater. Heavily in debt, Louisa is on a juggernaut that lands in her in the hospital, after she collapses from exhaustion.
1972. Drama. Director: Brian G. Hutton. Stars Elizabeth Taylor, Micheal Caine & Susannah York. The venomous wife of a wealthy architect tries, at length, to break up her husband and his mistress.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life In Good Shape
programmes.show_description: 1962. A focus on modern design in Britain, with a look at some of the recent exhibits at the Design Centre.
The Bride Possessed. 1959. Stars Virginia Leith, Skip Homeier & Harry Townes. Matt Conroy is startled when on their honeymoon his wife Sally suddenly is a determined woman, who denies knowing him.
